Go to data.hrsa.gov website and select "Query Data". Pick the Medically Underserved Areas/Populations (MUA/P) data source in the step 1 menu and select "View Data". Enter the name of the county (_______ county) in the first "Service Area" column, adding the state in the 5th column may help direct you to the data.  If the designation type in the third column is "medically underserved area" enter "Yes" as the value. If the county generates a "No data available in table" enter "No" as the value.  A value of "Unknown" indicates that sufficient data was not available to look up the value.  If value is yes, complete the Medically_underserved_score d | eg=["None"]

scATACseq_Workflow_Parameters_Description: TEXT | Parameters used to run the scATAC-seq workflow. | eg=["See paper Methods", "To integrate data from all patients, we first merged peaks called from all patients using the mergePeaks module of scATAC-pro. With this union set of peaks, we reconstructed the peak-by-cell count matrix for each patient using the reConstMtx module in scATAC- pro. The 18 peak-by-cell matrices were combined into a bigger peak-by-cell matrix and converted to a Seurat object. The data were next normalized using the TF-IDF normalization in scATAC-pro. Top 10,000 variably accessible peaks (VAPs) were identified using the FindVariableFeatures function in Seurat. We further filtered peaks that were accessible in fewer than 1% of the cells. TF-IDF normalization was then performed on the count matrix with only the VAPs. The PCA was performed on the VAPs and the cells were clustered using the first 50 PCs and the FindClusters function in Seurat with the resolution=0.4. To reduce batch effect, we reselect the VAPs across the clusters. To do so, we summarized the count matrix into a peak-by-clu..."]

## HTAN_VERSIONED.PUBLICATION_MANIFEST_METADATA_R5

Publication_Abstract: TEXT | The abstract or short description of the content presented/published. | eg=["Colorectal cancers (CRCs) arise from precursor polyps whose cellular origins, molecular heterogeneity, and immunogenic potential may reveal diagnostic and therapeutic insights when analyzed at high resolution. We present a single-cell transcriptomic and imaging atlas of the two most common human colorectal polyps, conventional adenomas and serrated polyps, and their resulting CRC counterparts. Integrative analysis of 128 datasets from 62 participants reveals adenomas arise from WNT-driven expansion of stem cells, while serrated polyps derive from differentiated cells through gastric metaplasia. Metaplasia-associated damage is coupled to a cytotoxic immune microenvironment preceding hypermutation, driven partly by antigen-presentation differences associated with tumor cell-differentiation status. Microsatellite unstable CRCs contain distinct non-metaplastic regions where tumor cells acquire stem cell properties and cytotoxic immune cells are depleted. Our multi-omic atlas provides in..."]

Example: 17p13.1. Note: This node is meant to capture molecular tests that were completed clinically for the participant and only includes data from diagnostic array that was completed prior to research sequencing was done. Do not include data related to research assay outputs here.

disease_ontology_term_id: TEXT | MONDO disease ontology ID (REF: https://www.ebi.ac.uk/ols/ontologies/mondo) (ex. MONDO:0100426 or PATO:0000461 for 'normal') | eg=["MONDO:0024660"]
assay: TEXT | An assay based on 10X Genomics. Example : 100x3’ v2 | eg=["single-cell RNA sequencing"]
assay_ontology_term_id: TEXT | Experimental Factor Ontology ID (REF: https://www.ebi.ac.uk/ols/ontologies/efo) | eg=["EFO:0008913"]
cell_type: TEXT | The type of cell (i.e. T-cell) | eg=["absorptive cell"]
development_stage: TEXT | Human development stage derived from HsapDv term  (Example if data is Homo sapiens, development stage is obtained from Link. HsapDv:0000114 - 20-year-old human stage) | eg=["59-year-old human stage"]
feature_reference: TEXT | A reference to the feature: NCBI Taxonomy ID | eg=["NCBITaxon:9606"]
author_cell_type: TEXT | ABS = absorptive cells, TUF = tuft cells, EE = enteroendocrine cells, ASC = adenoma specific cells, TAC = transient amplifying cells, CT = crypt top cells, GOB = goblet cells, SSC= serrated specific cells, STM = stem cells | eg=["ABS"]
sex: TEXT | Phenotypic sex | eg=["male"]
HTAN_Parent_Data_File_ID: TEXT | HTAN Data File Identifier indicating the file(s) from which these files were derived | eg=["HTA11_3361_200000101113311"]
iVar: NUMBER | Index to gene IDs | eg=[103]
feature_is_filtered: BOOLEAN | Indicates if the feature (gene) was/should be filtered in analysis | eg=[false]
iObs: NUMBER | Index to cell barcodes | eg=[24993, 24997]
is_primary_data: BOOLEAN | Indicates if the sample is primary or not (True/False) | eg=[true]
feature_biotype: TEXT | Biological type of feature (e.g. gene) | eg=["gene"]
suspension_type: TEXT | Cell, nucleus, or na, as corresponding to sample preparation | eg=["cell"]
organism: TEXT | The genus and species from which biosamples are derived | eg=["Homo sapiens"]
X_value: FLOAT | Expression value for a given gene and cell | eg=[2.710271120071411, -0.22273069620132446]
disease: TEXT | Description of disease (example: lung adenocarcinoma) or normal cells | eg=["tubular adenoma"]
tissue: TEXT | Derived from UBERON or CL terms that best describes the tissue that the cell was derived from. | eg=["ascending colon"]
feature_name: TEXT | Gene name from HUGO Human Gene Nomenclature Committee (https://www.genenames.org/) | eg=["ABHD15"]
HTAN_Specimen_ID: TEXT | HTAN ID associated with a biosample based on HTAN ID SOP (eg HTANx_yyy_zzz) | eg=["HTA11_3361_2000001011"]
self_reported_ethnicity: TEXT | Ethnicity of the individual (obtained from HANCESTRO terms for Homo sapiens) | eg=["European"]
self_reported_ethnicity_ontology_term_id: TEXT | If human, the Human Ancestry Ontology ID (i.e. HANCESTRO:0005) (REF: https://www.ebi.ac.uk/ols/ontologies/hancestro); "multiethnic" if more than one ethnicity. "Unknown" if human and no information, else "na" if non-human | eg=["HANCESTRO:0005"]
cell_type_ontology_term_id: TEXT | Cell Ontology ID (i.e. CL:0000784)  (REF: https://www.ebi.ac.uk/ols/ontologies/cl) | eg=["CL:0000212"]

With this union set of peaks, we reconstructed the peak-by-cell count matrix for each patient using the reConstMtx module in scATAC- pro. The 18 peak-by-cell matrices were combined into a bigger peak-by-cell matrix and converted to a Seurat object. The data were next normalized using the TF-IDF normalization in scATAC-pro. Top 10,000 variably accessible peaks (VAPs) were identified using the FindVariableFeatures function in Seurat. We further filtered peaks that were accessible in fewer than 1% of the cells. TF-IDF normalization was then performed on the count matrix with only the VAPs. The PCA was performed on the VAPs and the cells were clustered using the first 50 PCs and the FindClusters function in Seurat with the resolution=0.4. To reduce batch effect, we reselect the VAPs across the clusters. To do so, we summarized the count matrix into a peak-by-clu..."]

Slide areas are named consecutively from top to bottom: A1, B1, C1, D1 for Visium slides with 6.5 mm Capture Area and A, B for CytAssist slides with 11 mm Capture Area. Both CytAssist slides with 6.5 mm Capture Area and Gateway Slides contain only two slide areas, A1 and D1. | eg=["A1", "D1"]

## HTAN_VERSIONED.SCHEMA_R3
Required: TEXT | Indicate if specifying value for attribute X is mandatory or optional by default. Set to TRUE or FALSE. If TRUE, a data contributor must provide a value for attribute X (e.g. one of the valid values in 'Valid Values', if any are specified). If FALSE, a data contributor may select between 'Not Applicable' or 'Not Specified' as a value for attribute X. Note that an attribute may not be required by default (i.e. 'Required' is set to FALSE), but it could be required by virtue of being an attribute of another required attribute (e.g. see ERCC). | eg=["TRUE"]
DependsOn_Component: TEXT | Each component is a high level ontology class in which entities/objects are categorized/componentized; a component is an entity on its own that needs to be defined in the schema
Validation_Rules: TEXT | What type of data is allowed in the Valid Values column. Only relevant if Valid Values are not present in the row. E.g. string, list, integer
Properties: TEXT | Properties specific to a particular attribute; note that properties could be attributes themselves (i.e. listed under the 'Attributes' column).
Attribute: TEXT | Key characteristic X of the assay, related protocol, or downstream data that we want to record as metadata feature. | eg=["Library Construction Method", "Alignment Workflow Type"]
Parent: TEXT | Parent class of this attribute - i.e. where to 'hook' this attribute into an existing schema. Note that this assumes a single existing schema/a single json-ld schema context; if we need to support classes/attributes/properties defined across multiple schemas/json-ld contexts we'd need to add a schema/context column that specifies the context of each attribute, if different from the context of the attribute's parent. | eg=["Sequencing", "Biospecimen"]
DependsOn: TEXT | Important characteristics, if any, of attribute X that need to be recorded as metadata features given attribute X is specified. These characteristics could be attributes or properties of attributes (i.e. they are listed in the 'Attribute' column and/or under the 'Properties' column). Comma separated.
Source: TEXT | The source for the description of the term, usually an URI. Ontologies can be used as sources. E.g. Library Construction Method has source: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000711 | eg=["http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000711", "https://docs.gdc.cancer.gov/Data_Dictionary/viewer/#?view=table-definition-view&id=alignment_workflow"]
Valid_Values: TEXT | The range of attribute X: the values that can be assigned to attribute X; these values can include/be other attributes (i.e. listed in the 'Attribute' columns) themselves. Comma separated. If none provided any non-null value is valid for attribute X. | eg=["Smart-seq2, Smart-SeqV4, 10xV1.0, 10xV1.1, 10xV2, 10xV3,10xV3.1, CEL-seq2, Drop-seq, inDropsV2, inDropsV3, TruDrop, sci-ATAC-seq", "BWA, BWA with BQSR, BWA-aln, BWA-mem, BWA with Mark Duplicates and BQSR, STAR 2-Pass, STAR 2-Pass Chimeric, STAR 2-Pass Genome, STAR 2-Pass Transcriptome, Bowtie, Bismark, GSNAP, BSMAP, BSmooth, BS-Seeker2, BS-Seeker, RMAP, MethylCoder, BRAT-BW, Pash, Segemehl, Bisulfighter, BatMeth, LAST, ERNE-BS5, B-SOLANA, SOCS-B, BWA-meth, Other Alignment Workflow, None"]
Description: TEXT | Provide a definition or a reference containing the definition of attribute X. Preferably provide a source ontology link or code in addition to the definition. Leave blank if the final description is pending. | eg=["Process which results in the creation of a library from fragments of DNA using cloning vectors or oligonucleotides with the role of adaptors [OBI_0000711]", "Generic name for the workflow used to analyze a data set."]

## HTAN_VERSIONED.SCRNASEQ_VUMC_HTAN_VAL_EPI_CELLXGENE_R3
iObs: NUMBER | Index to cell barcodes | eg=[21876, 21877]
Polyp_Type: TEXT | TA = tubular adenoma, HP = hyperplastic polyp, TVA = tubulovillus adenoma, SSL = sessile serrated lesion, UNC = unclassified, NL = normal | eg=["NL"]
cell_type: TEXT | The type of cell (i.e. T-cell) | eg=["absorptive cell"]
iVar: NUMBER | Index to gene IDs | eg=[3836]
disease_ontology_term_id: TEXT | MONDO disease ontology ID (REF: https://www.ebi.ac.uk/ols/ontologies/mondo) (ex. MONDO:0100426 or PATO:0000461 for 'normal') | eg=["PATO:0000461"]
tissue: TEXT | Derived from UBERON or CL terms that best describes the tissue that the cell was derived from. | eg=["ascending colon"]
author_cell_type: TEXT | ABS = absorptive cells, TUF = tuft cells, EE = enteroendocrine cells, ASC = adenoma specific cells, TAC = transient amplifying cells, CT = crypt top cells, GOB = goblet cells, SSC= serrated specific cells, STM = stem cells | eg=["ABS"]
tissue_ontology_term_id: TEXT | Uber-anatomy ontology ID (REF: https://www.ebi.ac.uk/ols/ontologies/uberon) (ex.UBERON:2001190) | eg=["UBERON:0001156"]
feature_is_filtered: BOOLEAN | Indicates if the feature (gene) was/should be filtered in analysis | eg=[false]
is_primary_data: BOOLEAN | Indicates if the sample is primary or not (True/False) | eg=[true]
disease: TEXT | Description of disease (example: lung adenocarcinoma) or normal cells | eg=["normal"]
feature_biotype: TEXT | Biological type of feature (e.g. gene) | eg=["gene"]
feature_reference: TEXT | A reference to the feature: NCBI Taxonomy ID | eg=["NCBITaxon:9606"]
Sample_Classification: TEXT | AD = conventional adenoma, SER = serrated polyp, UNC = unclassified, NL = normal | eg=["NL"]
self_reported_ethnicity_ontology_term_id: TEXT | If human, the Human Ancestry Ontology ID (i.e. HANCESTRO:0005) (REF: https://www.ebi.ac.uk/ols/ontologies/hancestro); "multiethnic" if more than one ethnicity. "Unknown" if human and no information, else "na" if non-human | eg=["HANCESTRO:0005"]
suspension_type: TEXT | Cell, nucleus, or na, as corresponding to sample preparation | eg=["cell"]
development_stage_ontology_term_id: TEXT | Human Developmental Stages ID (REF: https://www.ebi.ac.uk/ols/ontologies/hsapdv) or Mouse Developmental Stages ID (REF: https://www.ebi.ac.uk/ols/ontologies/mmusdv) (HsapDv if human, MmusDv if mouse, unknown if information unavailable) | eg=["HsapDv:0000160"]
X_value: FLOAT | Expression value for a given gene and cell | eg=[-0.12030201405286789]
feature_name: TEXT | Gene name from HUGO Human Gene Nomenclature Committee (https://www.genenames.org/) | eg=["CAPN12"]
cell_type_ontology_term_id: TEXT | Cell Ontology ID (i.e. CL:0000784)  (REF: https://www.ebi.ac.uk/ols/ontologies/cl) | eg=["CL:0000212"]
HTAN_Specimen_ID: TEXT | HTAN ID associated with a biosample based on HTAN ID SOP (eg HTANx_yyy_zzz) | eg=["HTA11_8622_2000002011"]
sex_ontology_term_id: TEXT | Phenotype And Trait Ontology ID, PATO:0000384 for male, PATO:0000383 for female, or unknown if unavailable | eg=["PATO:0000383"]
donor_id: TEXT | Identifies a unique individual that data were derived from | eg=["HTA11_8622"]
HTAN_Parent_Data_File_ID: TEXT | HTAN Data File Identifier indicating the file(s) from which these files were derived | eg=["HTA11_8622_200000201113111"]
development_stage: TEXT | Human development stage derived from HsapDv term  (Example if data is Homo sapiens, development stage is obtained from Link. HsapDv:0000114 - 20-year-old human stage) | eg=["66-year-old human stage"]
self_reported_ethnicity: TEXT | Ethnicity of the individual (obtained from HANCESTRO terms for Homo sapiens) | eg=["European"]
assay: TEXT | An assay based on 10X Genomics. Example : 100x3’ v2 | eg=["single-cell RNA sequencing"]
assay_ontology_term_id: TEXT | Experimental Factor Ontology ID (REF: https://www.ebi.ac.uk/ols/ontologies/efo) | eg=["EFO:0008913"]
organism_ontology_term_id: TEXT | NCBITaxon ID (REF: https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/taxonomy) (ex. NCBITaxon:9606 for human, NCBITaxon:10090 for mouse) | eg=["NCBITaxon:9606"]
sex: TEXT | Phenotypic sex | eg=["female"]
organism: TEXT | The genus and species from which biosamples are derived | eg=["Homo sapiens"]

DESCRIPTION: TEXT | Provide a definition or a reference containing the definition of attribute X. Preferably provide a source ontology link or code in addition to the definition. Leave blank if the final description is pending. | eg=["Single-cell RNA-seq [EFO_0008913]", "Bulk RNA-seq [EFO_0003738]"]
REQUIRED: TEXT | Indicate if specifying value for attribute X is mandatory or optional by default. Set to TRUE or FALSE. If TRUE, a data contributor must provide a value for attribute X (e.g. one of the valid values in 'Valid Values', if any are specified). If FALSE, a data contributor may select between 'Not Applicable' or 'Not Specified' as a value for attribute X. Note that an attribute may not be required by default (i.e. 'Required' is set to FALSE), but it could be required by virtue of being an attribute of another required attribute (e.g. see ERCC). | eg=["FALSE", "TRUE"]
DEPENDSON_COMPONENT: TEXT | Each component is a high level ontology class in which entities/objects are categorized/componentized; a component is an entity on its own that needs to be defined in the schema | eg=["Biospecimen"]
ATTRIBUTE: TEXT | Key characteristic X of the assay, related protocol, or downstream data that we want to record as metadata feature. | eg=["scRNA-seq Level 1", "Bulk RNA-seq Level 1"]
VALIDATION_RULES: TEXT | What type of data is allowed in the Valid Values column. Only relevant if Valid Values are not present in the row. E.g. string, list, integer | eg=["num"]
PROPERTIES: TEXT | Properties specific to a particular attribute; note that properties could be attributes themselves (i.e. listed under the 'Attributes' column).
VALID_VALUES: TEXT | The range of attribute X: the values that can be assigned to attribute X; these values can include/be other attributes (i.e. listed in the 'Attribute' columns) themselves. Comma separated. If none provided any non-null value is valid for attribute X.
PARENT: TEXT | Parent class of this attribute - i.e. where to 'hook' this attribute into an existing schema. Note that this assumes a single existing schema/a single json-ld schema context; if we need to support classes/attributes/properties defined across multiple schemas/json-ld contexts we'd need to add a schema/context column that specifies the context of each attribute, if different from the context of the attribute's parent. | eg=["Sequencing", "Biospecimen"]

## HTAN_VERSIONED.SRRS_CLINICAL_TIER2_R2
Test_Result: TEXT | The text term used to describe the result of the molecular test. If the test result was a numeric value see test_value. Note: This node is meant to capture molecular tests that were completed clinically for the participant and only includes data from diagnostic array that was completed prior to research sequencing was done. Do not include data related to research assay outputs here. | eg=["Not Applicable"]

Note: if the event occurs at a single time point, e.g. a diagnosis or a lab test, the values for this column is 'Not Applicable' | eg=["None"]

entityId: TEXT | Synapse ID of entity | eg=["syn26464373"]

Race: TEXT | An arbitrary classification of a taxonomic group that is a division of a species. It usually arises as a consequence of geographical isolation withina a species and is characterized by shared heredity, physical attributes and behavior, and in the case of humans, by common history, nationality, or geographic distribution. | eg=["white"]

## HTAN_VERSIONED.SCHEMA_R4
Properties: TEXT | Properties specific to a particular attribute; note that properties could be attributes themselves (i.e. listed under the 'Attributes' column).
Parent: TEXT | Parent class of this attribute - i.e. where to 'hook' this attribute into an existing schema. Note that this assumes a single existing schema/a single json-ld schema context; if we need to support classes/attributes/properties defined across multiple schemas/json-ld contexts we'd need to add a schema/context column that specifies the context of each attribute, if different from the context of the attribute's parent. | eg=["scRNA-seq Level 1", "Sequencing"]
Validation_Rules: TEXT | What type of data is allowed in the Valid Values column. Only relevant if Valid Values are not present in the row. E.g. string, list, integer
Valid_Values: TEXT | The range of attribute X: the values that can be assigned to attribute X; these values can include/be other attributes (i.e. listed in the 'Attribute' columns) themselves. Comma separated. If none provided any non-null value is valid for attribute X. | eg=["Oligo-dT, Poly-dT, Feature barcoding, Random", "Gene Expression, Transcript Expression, Exon Expression Quantification, Gene Expression Quantification, Isoform Expression Quantification, Splice Junction Quantification, Other"]
DependsOn: TEXT | Important characteristics, if any, of attribute X that need to be recorded as metadata features given attribute X is specified. These characteristics could be attributes or properties of attributes (i.e. they are listed in the 'Attribute' column and/or under the 'Properties' column). Comma separated.
Source: TEXT | The source for the description of the term, usually an URI. Ontologies can be used as sources. E.g. Library Construction Method has source: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0000711 | eg=["http://www.sequenceontology.org/so_wiki/index.php/Category:SO:0000112_!_primer", "https://docs.gdc.cancer.gov/Data_Dictionary/viewer/#?view=table-definition-view&id=gene_expression"]
Required: TEXT | Indicate if specifying value for attribute X is mandatory or optional by default. Set to TRUE or FALSE. If TRUE, a data contributor must provide a value for attribute X (e.g. one of the valid values in 'Valid Values', if any are specified). If FALSE, a data contributor may select between 'Not Applicable' or 'Not Specified' as a value for attribute X. Note that an attribute may not be required by default (i.e. 'Required' is set to FALSE), but it could be required by virtue of being an attribute of another required attribute (e.g. see ERCC). | eg=["TRUE"]
DependsOn_Component: TEXT | Each component is a high level ontology class in which entities/objects are categorized/componentized; a component is an entity on its own that needs to be defined in the schema
Description: TEXT | Provide a definition or a reference containing the definition of attribute X. Preferably provide a source ontology link or code in addition to the definition. Leave blank if the final description is pending. | eg=["An oligo to which new deoxyribonucleotides can be added by DNA polymerase [SO_0000112]. This allows users to identify content of the cDNA library input e.g. enriched for mRNA", "Specific content type of the data file."]

Acquisition_Method_Other_Specify: TEXT | A custom acquisition method [Text - max length 100 characters] | eg=["Needle aspiration", "Paracentesis"]
Tumor_Infiltrating_Lymphocytes: TEXT | Measure of Tumor-Infiltrating Lymphocytes [Number]
Histology_Assessment_Medium: TEXT | The method of assessment used to characterize histology
Adjacent_Biospecimen_IDs: TEXT | List of HTAN Identifiers (separated by commas) of adjacent biospecimens cut from the same sample; for example HTA3_3000_3, HTA3_3000_4, ...
Sectioning_Days_from_Index: TEXT | Number of days from the research participant's index date that the biospecimen was sectioned after collection. If not applicable please enter 'Not Applicable'
Protocol_Link: TEXT | Protocols.io ID or DOI link to a free/open protocol resource describing in detail the assay protocol (e.g. surface markers used in Smart-seq, dissociation duration,  lot/batch numbers for key reagents such as primers, sequencing reagent kits, etc.) or the protocol by which the sample was obtained or generated. | eg=["dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bjyzkpx6", "Not Available"]
Percent_Stromal_Cells: TEXT | Numeric value to represent the percentage of reactive cells that are present in a malignant tumor sample or specimen but are not malignant such as fibroblasts, vascular structures, etc.
Id: TEXT | Unique identifier of entity e.g. a96059eb-81af-4b24-ae50-9242c0d8f819 | eg=["4b7d9a6b-0529-4763-bd9d-fce742bc2c24", "9ccd07ce-bb78-4f21-aaae-24f8e99130b3"]
HTAN_Center: TEXT | Name of HTAN Center e.g. HTAN HTAPP | eg=["HTAN MSK", "HTAN HTAPP"]
Preinvasive_Morphology: TEXT | Histologic Morphology not included in ICD-O-3 morphology codes, for preinvasive lesions included in the HTAN
Mounting_Medium: TEXT | The solution in which the specimen is embedded, generally under a cover glass. It may be liquid, gum or resinous, soluble in water, alcohol or other solvents and be sealed from the external atmosphere by non-soluble ringing media
Preservation_Method: TEXT | Text term that represents the method used to preserve the sample. | eg=["Fresh dissociated and single cell sorted", "Fresh"]
Percent_Tumor_Nuclei: TEXT | Numeric value to represent the percentage of tumor nuclei in a malignant neoplasm sample or specimen.
Analyte_Type: TEXT | The kind of molecular specimen analyte: a molecular derivative (I.e. RNA / DNA / Protein Lysate) obtained from a specimen
Site_of_Resection_or_Biopsy: TEXT | The text term used to describe the anatomic site of the resection or biopsy of the patient's malignant disease, as described by the World Health Organization's (WHO) International Classification of Diseases for Oncology (ICD-O). | eg=["Pleura NOS", "Other ill-defined sites"]
Percent_Inflam_Infiltration: TEXT | Numeric value to represent local response to cellular injury, marked by capillary dilatation, edema and leukocyte infiltration; clinically, inflammation is manifest by redness, heat, pain, swelling and loss of function, with the need to heal damaged tissue.
Collection_Media: TEXT | Material Specimen is collected into post procedure
Total_Volume: TEXT | Numeric value for the total amount of sample or specimen
Percent_Normal_Cells: TEXT | Numeric value to represent the percentage of normal cell content in a malignant tumor sample or specimen.
Slide_Charge_Type: TEXT | A description of the charge on the glass slide.
entityId: TEXT | Synapse ID of entity | eg=["syn24983234", "syn27208165"]
Biospecimen_Dimension_3: TEXT | Third dimension of tissue fragment (number, up to one decimal place) measured in units as defined by the "dimensions_unit" CDE

Enter the name of the county (______ county) in the first "Service Area" column, adding the state in the 5th column may help direct you to the data. Enter the Index of Medical Underservice Score in the fourth column to one decimal place as the value.

SCATACSEQ_WORKFLOW_PARAMETERS_DESCRIPTION: TEXT | NULL | eg=["To integrate data from all patients, we first merged peaks called from all patients using the mergePeaks module of scATAC-pro. With this union set of peaks, we reconstructed the peak-by-cell count matrix for each patient using the reConstMtx module in scATAC- pro. The 18 peak-by-cell matrices were combined into a bigger peak-by-cell matrix and converted to a Seurat object. The data were next normalized using the TF-IDF normalization in scATAC-pro. Top 10,000 variably accessible peaks (VAPs) were identified using the FindVariableFeatures function in Seurat. We further filtered peaks that were accessible in fewer than 1% of the cells. TF-IDF normalization was then performed on the count matrix with only the VAPs. The PCA was performed on the VAPs and the cells were clustered using the first 50 PCs and the FindClusters function in Seurat with the resolution=0.4. To reduce batch effect, we reselect the VAPs across the clusters. To do so, we summarized the count matrix into a peak-by-clu..."]
